identity.name: Standard
identity.definition: A Standard is a grade-level learning expectation from a state or district-adopted framework that organizes and groups the ROCK Skills students are measured against, forming the primary column axis of the Skills Grid.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Standard appears in grids and browsing views

attributes.name: Standard Name
attributes.description: Display name of the standard. Appears as the primary label on cards and grid columns.

attributes.name: Domain
attributes.description: Grouping category within the framework (e.g., Reading Informational Text, Number & Operations).

attributes.name: Grade Level
attributes.description: Target grade level. Determines which Skills Grid the standard appears in.

attributes.name: Subject
attributes.description: Subject area: ELA or Math. Determines visual color coding.

attributes.name: Skill Count
attributes.description: Number of Skills grouped under this Standard.

representations.heading: List layout
representations.description: How a Standard appears in Skills Grid columns and tables

attributes.name: Skill Count
attributes.description: Number of Skills grouped under this Standard.

attributes.name: Expanded/Collapsed State
attributes.description: Whether the standard column is expanded to show individual Skills.

representations.heading: Detail page layout
representations.description: Full Standard view with nested Skills and proficiency distribution

attributes.name: Standard ID
attributes.description: Unique identifier from the adopted framework (e.g., 3.RI.1, 4.OA.2).

attributes.name: Skill Count
attributes.description: Number of Skills grouped under this Standard.

attributes.name: Expanded/Collapsed State
attributes.description: Whether the standard column is expanded to show individual Skills.

stories.title: View Standards Grid
stories.benefit: I can see all Standards as columns in the Skills Grid and understand which learning expectations my students are measured against.
stories.whenClause: I open the Skills Grid for my class,
stories.thenClause: each Standard appears as a column header, with its name and the number of Skills it contains.

stories.title: Expand Standard for Skills
stories.benefit: I can see the individual Skills grouped under it and review per-student proficiency at the Skill level.
stories.whenClause: I click on a Standard column header,
stories.thenClause: the column expands to reveal each Skill within the Standard as a sub-column, showing individual Skill proficiency cells.

stories.title: Filter Standards by Domain
stories.benefit: I can focus on a specific domain within the framework and reduce visual clutter in the grid.
stories.whenClause: I select a domain filter (e.g., 'Reading Informational Text'),
stories.thenClause: the grid shows only Standards belonging to that domain, hiding unrelated columns.

businessRules.title: Framework Source
businessRules.description: Standards are imported from state or district-adopted frameworks (e.g., Common Core, TEKS, state-specific standards). They are not manually created.

businessRules.title: Uniqueness
businessRules.description: A Standard is uniquely identified by its Standard ID within a given framework and grade level. The same Standard ID may appear across different grade levels.

businessRules.title: Skill Grouping
businessRules.description: Every Skill must belong to exactly one Standard. A Standard contains one or more Skills. The Standard → Skill relationship defines the column structure of the Skills Grid.

businessRules.title: Subject Binding
businessRules.description: A Standard is bound to exactly one subject area (ELA or Math). Cross-subject Standards are not supported; each subject has its own framework hierarchy.

businessRules.title: Grade-Level Scope
businessRules.description: Standards are scoped to a specific grade level within a framework. The same conceptual standard may have different IDs and expectations at different grade levels.

businessRules.title: Grid Column Axis
businessRules.description: Standards form the primary column axis of the Skills Grid. Each Standard occupies one column (collapsed) or multiple sub-columns (expanded, one per Skill).

businessRules.title: Expand/Collapse Behavior
businessRules.description: When collapsed, a Standard column shows an aggregate proficiency indicator. When expanded, individual Skill columns appear with per-student proficiency cells.

businessRules.title: Deprecation
businessRules.description: When a framework is updated or replaced, Standards from the old framework are marked Deprecated. Deprecated Standards retain historical data but no longer appear in active Skills Grids.

businessRules.title: Domain Filtering
businessRules.description: Standards are organized into Domains within a framework. Domain-level filtering allows teachers to focus the Skills Grid on a subset of related Standards.

states.name: Active
states.description: Currently part of the adopted framework and visible in Skills Grids

states.name: Deprecated
states.description: Superseded by a newer framework version; historical data retained
states.triggers: Framework update, state/district framework adoption change

relationships.mechanics: Skills are grouped under Standards based on the adopted framework. The mapping is imported from the framework definition and is not editable by users.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A Standard contains one or more Skills; each Skill belongs to exactly one Standard. Typical: 3-8 skills per standard.
relationships.sorts: Skills within a Standard: by Skill ID (default), by proficiency level, alphabetical by name.
relationships.filters: By proficiency level (mastered, developing, not started), by assessment recency.
relationships.dependencies: If a Standard is deprecated, its Skills are also deprecated. Skill proficiency data persists for historical reporting.

relationships.mechanics: Students are measured against Standards via their Skill proficiency data. The intersection of Student × Standard produces aggregate proficiency in the Skills Grid.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many (indirect via Skills). All Students in a class are measured against the same set of grade-level Standards.
relationships.sorts: Students within a Standard column: by proficiency level (default), alphabetical by name.
relationships.filters: By proficiency level (above, at, below), by class, by demographic group.
relationships.dependencies: Student proficiency data for a Standard persists even if the student changes classes or schools.

nestedObjects.name: Skill
nestedObjects.description: Individual measurable skills grouped under this Standard

nestedObjects.name: Proficiency Prediction
nestedObjects.description: AI-generated predictions of student proficiency for this Standard

nestedObjects.name: Insight
nestedObjects.description: System-generated actionable insights about class performance on this Standard

allAttributes.name: Standard ID
allAttributes.description: Unique identifier from the adopted framework (e.g., 3.RI.1, 4.OA.2). Used for cross-system matching and reporting.

allAttributes.name: Standard Name
allAttributes.description: Display name of the standard. Appears as the primary label on cards, grid columns, and detail views.

allAttributes.name: Domain
allAttributes.description: Grouping category within the framework (e.g., Reading Informational Text, Number & Operations). Used for domain-level filtering.

allAttributes.name: Grade Level
allAttributes.description: Target grade level. Determines which Skills Grid the standard appears in. PreK-12.

allAttributes.name: Subject
allAttributes.description: Subject area: ELA or Math. Determines visual color coding and framework hierarchy.

allAttributes.name: Skill Count
allAttributes.description: Number of Skills grouped under this Standard. Auto-calculated from framework mapping.

allAttributes.name: Expanded/Collapsed State
allAttributes.description: Whether the standard column is expanded to show individual Skill sub-columns. Defaults to collapsed. Per-user, per-session.

structure.evidence: A Standard has meaningful attributes: Standard ID, name, domain, grade level, subject, skill count, and expand/collapse state. You can design a detail view and grid column for a Standard.

instances.evidence: 3.RI.1 — Key Ideas and Details (Grade 3 ELA), 4.OA.2 — Operations & Algebraic Thinking (Grade 4 Math), K.RF.1 — Foundational Skills (Kindergarten ELA). Hundreds of Standard instances per framework.

purpose.evidence: Standards are the organizing structure of the Skills Grid. Teachers expand Standards to see Skill-level proficiency, filter by Domain to focus instruction, and use Standard-level aggregations to identify areas of strength and weakness.

sipValidation.verdict: Core knowledge object. The primary column axis of the Skills Grid and the structural parent of Skills.

synonyms.notes: Generic term used across multiple state frameworks
Sometimes used interchangeably; in Renaissance context, Standard is preferred
More granular than Standard; closer to Skill in Renaissance taxonomy

shapeshifterMatrix.description: A standard shown as a column header in the skills proficiency grid. Teachers expand or collapse standards to focus on specific skill areas.
Standards listed with domain, grade level, and skill count for planning instruction. Teachers choose which standards to focus on and filter by domain.
Standards shown as data rows within an assessment's alignment section. Teachers verify which standards an assessment covers.
A standard displayed as a tag chip within Freckle content, indicating which standard the practice activity addresses.
